title: Procedural sedation documentation prompts
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.
examination.rationale: The BSG section supports recovery and information-exchange documentation only for gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The current ASA standard supports documenting oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain, and other recovery observations without generating findings or treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

exact_section.heading: Standard III — continual evaluation and recovery record

medication.text: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.
medication.rationale: The intraoperative documentation section was reviewed for staff, monitoring, medicines, technique, and events, but no corresponding specific legacy item existed for mapping.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care

follow_up.text: Supports accurate, complete, and legible anesthesia documentation across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
follow_up.rationale: The ASA statement supports complete documentation across anesthesia-provider procedural and monitored-anesthesia care without supplying clinical content.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports accurate, complete, and legible anesthesia documentation across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care

source_limitations: Indication, procedure, consent, fasting, risk assessment, staff, sedative and dose, route, oxygen, airway, monitoring values, events, interventions, response, recovery, discharge, escort, instructions, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
